title: crataegus × media f. laciniata
intro: Crataegus × media f. laciniata is a hybrid hawthorn plant, resulting from the cross between the Midland hawthorn (Crataegus laevigata) and the May hawthorn (Crataegus monogyna). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health. The health benefits associated with Crataegus × media f. laciniata include improved cardiovascular function, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced antioxidant activity, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also available in combination with other herbs to support overall well-being.
